fullArticle:
Nathan Fillion’s new HBO show brings together the best parts of his biggest TV projects to date – The Rookie and Firefly – and it’s a more important show than it might seem. Nathan Fillion has had an impressive acting career in both film and TV, with some of his TV projects found among the best TV shows of all time. Fillion’s career began on TV in 1993, and his big break arrived in 2002 when he starred as Captain Mal Reynolds in the sci-fi classic Firefly.

Despite its early cancellation, Firefly is now one of the best sci-fi TV shows of all time, and it opened many doors for Fillion. After movies like Slither and Super, and TV shows like Desperate Housewives and Castle, Fillion’s career found new life with the police procedural TV series The Rookie.
